channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: An influx of outsiders and money turns Montana Republican, culminating in a Senate triumph
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — Democrats' crushing loss in Montana's nationally important U.S. Senate race settled a fierce political debate over whether a surge of newcomers in the past decade favored Republicans — and if one of the new arrivals could even take high office.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From CDC to labor secretary: See Trump's top picks for Cabinet roles
item.description: Trump announced a slew of cabinet announcement on Friday as he begins to shape his Cabinet for his second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump picks Dr. Janette Nesheiwat as nation's next surgeon general
item.description: President-elect Trump has nominated Dr. Janette Nesheiwat as surgeon general, calling her a "fierce advocate and strong communicator for preventative medicine and public health.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's how the US and Israel could thwart Iran’s nuclear efforts under a new Trump administration
item.description: President-elect Trump will take office just as Iran has the potential to become the world’s 10th nuclear-armed state, and it’s not clear if either side knows how it will approach the other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge reduces prison sentence for Capitol rioter who berated and insulted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday imposed a one-year reduction in a prison sentence for a man who stormed the U.S. Capitol and then engaged in a pattern of disruptive courtroom behavior, including berating and insulting the judge.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Applicants sue Minnesota’s Office of Cannabis Management over disqualification
item.description: The OCM turned to a lottery because more than 1,800 applicants had sought 282 licenses. This week, regulators disqualified two-thirds of the applicants for a variety of reasons.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Gaetz's status in the House and the Ethics Committee report
item.description: Matt Gaetz withdrew his name from Trump's nomination of attorney general on Thursday, and now the future for him in Congress is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEC Chair Gary Gensler, who took aim at crypto industry, to step down in January
item.description: Gensler said he would step down on Jan. 20, when Donald Trump will inaugurated as the next president. Gensler took big enforcement actions against the crypto industry.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Mississippi's judicial runoff elections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Voters in central Mississippi and the state’s Delta and Gulf Coast areas will return to the polls Tuesday to resolve two judicial races in which no candidate received the vote majority needed in the Nov. 5 general election to avoid a runoff.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here’s how Trump and the GOP may try to weaken Obamacare
item.description: Republican control of the White House and Congress sets the stage for potentially seismic changes including curtailing Medicaid, weakening patient protections, and increasing premium costs for millions.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Armour, Sam Whitehead, and Julie Rovner, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Pam Bondi for next attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump has named former Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi as his next nominee to lead the Department of Justice (DOJ). Bondi was a swift pick just hours after former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew himself from consideration for the role.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) political future is up in the air following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Tabke wins Shakopee-area legislative race after recount, House control still unknown
item.description: The results are unofficial because four ballots are still in dispute. Tabke’s margin of victory widened by one vote from an earlier count.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Minnesota Lottery needs to tighten up how it regulates retailers
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of the Legislative Auditor reports that the Minnesota State Lottery generally complies with requirements, but has room to improve when it comes to how it manages retailers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Matt Gaetz withdraws from attorney general nomination
item.description: Former Florida congressman Matt Gaetz said he is no longer seeking the position of U.S. attorney general Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Donald Trump Cabinet will have zero policy input
item.description: Bill O'Reilly tells NewsNation's "CUOMO" that Trump just wants people in his Cabinet that will do exactly as he tells them to do.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith: Trump may have ulterior motive for Gaetz pick
item.description: Stephen A. Smith says that “under normal circumstances” Matt Gaetz would never be confirmed as the nation's new attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: California judge rules Huntington Beach can proceed with voter ID law
item.description: A judge on Friday rejected California’s lawsuit against the city of Huntington Beach over a local measure allowing officials to require voter identification at the polls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk's DOGE commission considering mobile tax filing app: Report
item.description: The new DOGE commission is considering developing a free mobile app for tax filing, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s hunt for Treasury secretary down to the wire
item.description: The jockeying for President-elect Trump’s pick to run the all-important Treasury Department is coming down to the wire. Trump’s economic team will be tasked with imposing an aggressive tariff regime meant to shake up global trade while keeping financial markets calm amid the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Matthew Whitaker for ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump tapped Matthew Whitaker as his ambassador to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, which Trump has long criticized for taking advantage of U.S. defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Stephen Fowler, MPR News
